channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: IPv6 may briefly have accounted for more than half of internet traffic
item.description: Has IPv6 finally reached its day of glory? It’s fair to say that IPv6 has not had the level of take-up expected when the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) ratified it back in 1998. Take-up has been agonizingly slow, not reaching 5 percent of traffic until 2014.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Oracle delivers semantic search without LLMs
item.description: Oracle says its new Trusted Answer Search can deliver reliable results at scale in the enterprise by scouring a governed set of approved documents using vector search instead of large language models (LLMs) and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech company cuts 1,000 jobs in AI-driven restructuring
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter gives readers the latest AI technology advancements, covering the challenges and opportunities AI presents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The stigma around AI in journalism may be easing, but trust is still fragile
item.description: I tend to write about AI from the perspective of the bleeding edge, looking at how journalists and media companies are using the technology to change the way they work, reach new audiences, and transform their organizations.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: When cloud giants neglect resilience
item.description: In a recent article chronicling the history of Microsoft Azure and its intensifying woes, we see a narrative that has been building throughout the industry for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Inside NTT Research’s push to commercialize deep tech
item.description: Since opening in Silicon Valley in 2019, NTT Research has operated as a long-horizon science lab, a dedicated arm of Japan’s telecommunications giant NTT Group, which invests more than $3 billion annually in global R&D.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: AI isn’t built for all languages and cultures. There’s a push to fix that
item.description: Egyptian coder Assem Sabry has long wanted an AI model that represents his culture. The problem is he hasn’t been able to find one. “The AI industry in Egypt . . . doesn’t exist,” Sabry says. So he built his own: Horus, named after the ancient Egyptian god of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Younger adult colon cancer deaths are concentrated in people with less education, study says
item.description: A new study finds the worrisome rise in colorectal cancer deaths among younger adults is concentrated in people with less education.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GitHub adds Stacked PRs to speed complex code reviews
item.description: AI-aided development tools are churning out more lines of code than ever, presenting a challenge for reviewers who must review ever larger pull requests.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why your home Wi-Fi needs more than just a strong password
item.description: A strong Wi-Fi password is not enough to keep your online activity private. Learn why a VPN is essential to encrypt your connection and block ISP tracking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fixing encryption isn’t enough. Quantum developments put focus on authentication
item.description: We are now entering the era of fault-tolerant quantum computing. The computers are getting better. The qubits are getting faster and more reliable, and there are more of them.
item.dc:creator: Network World
